note: The product is intentionally framed as a local-first classroom operations copilot rather than a student chatbot.
The current stack uses dual-tier Gemma routing with live and planning model tiers.
The product currently uses multimodal worksheet extraction and image handling in the local/Ollama path.
Real Gemma 4 validation is not yet fully evidenced because the checked-in eval baseline remains pending.
Generic teacher-AI features such as differentiation, translation, and family communication are already crowded-market categories.
The product is more differentiated at the system/workflow-composition level than at the single-feature level.
Gemma 4 officially supports reasoning, multimodal inputs, long context, and native function-calling/structured JSON workflows.
The app is Gemma-centered today but does not yet exploit the broader Gemma variant stack that could strengthen the "Gemma-native" claim.
Alberta's classroom-complexity context makes local, collaborative, privacy-sensitive teacher/EA tooling unusually relevant.
The official Alberta complexity-team allocation list shows 118 allocations for Calgary School Division.
Multilingual demand, support complexity, and AI-readiness caution together create both need and adoption friction.
The platform could become highly valuable in Alberta if it proves coordination-time savings and handoff quality improvements, but current evidence is not yet sufficient to call it game-changing.
